Description: The scientific name of the enzyme, such as ALA dehydratase or Protoporphyrinogen oxidase.
The generic name of the medication, such as Remoxipride or Lurasidone.
The specific porphyria disorder associated with the enzyme, such as variegate porphyria.
Indicates whether the medication has received FDA approval, with values such as Yes or No.
Primary key uniquely identifying each enzyme record.
Describes the nature of the interaction between the medicine and enzyme, such as activitor or inhibitor.
Primary key uniquely identifying each medicine record.
Foreign key referencing the medicine table id column and part of the composite primary key identifying the interaction record.
The cellular compartment where the enzyme is located, such as Cytosol or Mitochondrion.
The Online Mendelian Inheritance in Man database identifier number associated with the enzyme or condition.
The chemical substance produced by the enzymatic reaction.
The chromosomal locus associated with the gene encoding the enzyme.
Foreign key referencing the enzyme table id column and part of the composite primary key identifying the interaction record.
The commercial brand name of the medication, such as Roxiam or Latuda.
